question.text: Under the Depositories Act, the depository as a registered owner of securities shall have:

answer.text: Limited voting rights as specified by SEBI from time to time
No voting rights or any other rights in respect of securities held by it
Full voting rights on behalf of the beneficial owner
Voting rights only in matters relating to the depository operations

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Depositories Act, the depository shall be deemed to be the registered owner for the purpose of effecting transfer of ownership. However, the depository as a registered owner shall not have any voting rights or any other rights in respect of securities held by it. All rights belong to the beneficial owner. **The Trap:** While the depository is the 'registered owner', this is only a legal fiction for facilitating transfers. All substantive rights including voting remain with the beneficial owner. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The depository holds securities in a fiduciary capacity - it is the registered owner only for procedural purposes. The beneficial owner retains all economic and governance rights.
